bullet_points: Hardcode all GEMS (z_obs=9.625) and Sparkler (z_obs=1.4) GC data (names/IDs, masses, ages, age errors lower/upper, metallicities) directly in the script
Initialize Planck 2018 cosmology (H0=67.4, Omega_m=0.315, Omega_Lambda=0.685) using astropy
For each GC, compute the lookback time at z_obs, subtract the cluster age to get the lookback time at formation, then solve for z_form using astropy's z_at_value with an upper bound of z=50
If the resulting lookback time at formation is negative, zero, or exceeds the age of the universe, flag that cluster with a printed warning and assign NaN to z_form, z_form_lo, z_form_hi
Propagate age errors (lower and upper) to compute z_form_lo (using age + age_err_hi) and z_form_hi (using age - age_err_lo), handling edge cases gracefully
Print a detailed table of all GC names, system labels, observed redshifts, ages, and computed z_form with uncertainties
Save the results to a CSV file with columns: system, name, z_obs, age_Gyr, log10_mass, ZH, z_form, z_form_lower_bound, z_form_upper_bound
sub_tasks.sub_task: Cosmological Temporal Mapping — Compute formation redshifts for GEMS and Sparkler GCs

bullet_points: Implement the Sheth-Tormen halo mass function using Planck 2018 cosmological parameters (H0=67.4, Omega_m=0.315, sigma8=0.811, ns=0.965)

Define the atomic cooling halo mass threshold M_min(z) corresponding to T_vir >= 10^4 K using the standard virial temperature–mass relation that accounts for mean molecular weight (mu~0.59 for ionized gas) and the redshift-dependent critical density; print M_min values at z=5, 10, 15 to verify they fall in the expected 10^7–10^8 solar mass range
Compute the comoving number density of atomic cooling halos by integrating the Sheth-Tormen mass function above M_min(z) over a redshift grid from z=2 to z=20
Multiply by the comoving volume element dV/dz to get the GC formation rate per unit redshift
Normalize the resulting curve to form a probability density function (PDF) and compute the cumulative distribution function (CDF) over the redshift range
Print the peak redshift of the theoretical PDF
Save the redshift array, normalized PDF, and CDF to a NPZ file for use in Steps 3 and 4
sub_tasks.sub_task: Atomic Cooling Model Reconstruction — Compute the Trenti et al. (2015) theoretical GC formation rate PDF and CDF

bullet_points: Load the formation redshifts CSV from Step 1 and the theoretical PDF/CDF NPZ from Step 2

Perform a one-sample K-S test comparing the GEMS z_form median values against the theoretical Trenti et al. (2015) CDF; print the K-S statistic, p-value, and an explicit note about the limited sample size (N=19)
Perform a two-sample K-S test comparing the GEMS and Sparkler z_form distributions; print the K-S statistic, p-value, and a note about the very small Sparkler sample (N=5)
Compute the Spearman rank correlation between [Z/H] and z_form for the combined GEMS+Sparkler sample and separately for each system; print rho and p-value for each

Compute the statistical summary table: mean, median, and standard deviation of age_Gyr, log10_mass, ZH, and z_form for GEMS, Sparkler, and the combined sample; print the table in a well-formatted manner
Save all statistical results (K-S stats, p-values, Spearman results) to a CSV file and the summary table to a separate CSV file
sub_tasks.sub_task: Statistical tests, Spearman correlation, and statistical summary table

bullet_points: Load all computed data from Steps 1, 2, and 3

Figure 1 (formation redshift comparison, 2-panel): Top panel shows the theoretical Trenti et al. (2015) GC formation rate PDF vs. redshift as a filled curve, with GEMS z_form values shown as vertical tick marks or scatter points with horizontal error bars, and Sparkler z_form values similarly marked in a different color; bottom panel shows the theoretical CDF with the empirical CDFs of GEMS and Sparkler z_form overplotted

Figure 2 (three-panel combined): Panel A: scatter plot of [Z/H] vs. log10(M/Msolar) for GEMS and Sparkler with metallicity error bars, plus a reference GC MZR line following Harris et al. (approximately [Z/H] = 0.4 * log10(M/Msolar) - 3.0); Panel B: scatter plot of [Z/H] vs. z_form for the combined sample with error bars, color-coded by system; Panel C: overlapping KDE or histogram of log10(M/Msolar) for GEMS and Sparkler, annotated with the two-sample K-S test result
Save Figure 1 and Figure 2 as separate high-resolution PNG files
sub_tasks.sub_task: Generate all analysis plots — two multi-panel figure files

bullet_points: Write a comprehensive Results section covering all analysis steps

Report the formation redshift mapping: describe the z_form values for GEMS GCs (range, mean, median, consistency with z_obs~9.6) and Sparkler GCs (range, mean, median, implied ancient formation epochs)

Report the atomic cooling model reconstruction: describe the theoretical PDF shape, the numerical peak redshift printed in Step 2, and the width of the distribution; quantitatively compare the peak redshift to the mean and median z_form of the GEMS sample and state the offset in redshift units
Report the K-S test results with exact statistics from Step 3, including the explicit caveats about sample size limitations
Report the Spearman rank correlation results (rho and p-value) for the combined sample and each system separately, and interpret the sign and magnitude physically
Report the mass-metallicity relation findings: describe the locus of both systems relative to the Harris et al. GC MZR reference line, and discuss any offset

Explicitly address the "metallicity paradox": discuss whether the near-solar or mildly sub-solar metallicities of some GEMS clusters (e.g., B2 with [Z/H]=+0.04) are consistent with formation in atomic cooling halos at z~10, or whether they suggest pre-enrichment or rapid in-situ enrichment
Report the mass distribution comparison: describe the two-sample K-S test result and discuss selection effects and dynamical evolution as explanations for the mass distribution differences
Report the statistical summary table values (mean, median, std for each system) from Step 3
Discuss consistency and tension with LCDM predictions, addressing all four key questions from the problem statement
Reference all generated plots by their descriptive names (Figure 1: formation redshift comparison; Figure 2: combined mass-metallicity, z_form-metallicity, and mass distribution panels)
sub_tasks.sub_task: Write the full Results section of the paper
